title: cornus stricta var. asperifolia
intro: Cornus stricta var. asperifolia, also known as rough-leafed dogwood, is a variant of the Cornus stricta species, a deciduous shrub native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cornus stricta var. asperifolia are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
